subject: English
chapter: Chapter 5: The Snake and the Mirror
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – The Snake and the Mirror
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: The chapter introduces a man who looks at himself in the mirror every day and is fascinated by his appearance. He admires his reflection and feels proud of himself.
questions: Who is the main character of the story?
What does he do every day?
How does he feel about his reflection?
Is he proud or humble?
What habit does he have regarding the mirror?

passages.paragraph: One day, he sees a snake behind him in the mirror and is terrified. He believes the snake is real and reacts with fear and panic.
questions: What does he see in the mirror?
How does he react to it?
Does he believe the snake is real?
What emotion does he feel?
Was he prepared for this sight?

passages.paragraph: The man tries to escape from the snake by moving away quickly. His fear increases as he imagines the snake attacking him.
questions: What does the man do when he sees the snake?
Does his fear increase or decrease?
What does he imagine the snake doing?
Does he try to face the snake?
How does panic affect him?

passages.paragraph: As he continues to panic, he realizes that the snake is actually his own reflection in the mirror. The fear was caused by his misunderstanding and imagination.
questions: What does he finally realize?
Was the snake real?
Why did he feel fear?
What caused his panic?
Did his imagination play a role?

passages.paragraph: The chapter shows how vanity and obsession with appearance can distract a person from reality. The man’s focus on himself made him misinterpret the situation.
questions: What does the chapter illustrate about vanity?
How did his obsession affect him?
Why did he misinterpret reality?
Was his focus on others or himself?
What lesson is shown about self-obsession?

passages.paragraph: The man eventually laughs at his own foolishness after understanding the truth. He realizes how imagination and fear can mislead people.
questions: How does the man react after realizing the truth?
Does he feel embarrassed or angry?
What does he learn about imagination?
What does the story teach about fear?
Does he take the lesson seriously?

passages.paragraph: The story highlights human psychology and the effect of fear on perception. Even an ordinary situation can seem dangerous if one is anxious or self-absorbed.
questions: What aspect of human behavior is highlighted?
How does fear affect perception?
Can ordinary situations seem dangerous?
What causes the man’s anxiety?
Does self-absorption play a role?

passages.paragraph: The snake in the mirror is a metaphor for inner fears and insecurities. The man’s reaction shows how external appearances can trigger internal emotions.
questions: What does the snake represent?
Is it real or metaphorical?
How are inner fears shown?
What triggers the man’s emotions?
Does the story deal with psychological insight?

passages.paragraph: Through humor and irony, the story engages readers while teaching lessons about vanity, fear, and human nature. It encourages self-awareness and reflection.
questions: What literary techniques are used in the story?
How does humor affect the reader?
What lessons are taught?
Does it encourage self-awareness?
How does irony play a role?

passages.paragraph: Overall, 'The Snake and the Mirror' shows that people should not be obsessed with appearance and should control imagination and fear to understand reality clearly.
questions: What is the overall message of the chapter?
Why should people control imagination?
How does obsession affect understanding?
What role does fear play?
What does the story teach about reality?
